description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's background compilation path due to an unsafe cross-thread handle dereference in Scope::NewHomeObjectVariableProxy (src/ast/scopes.cc). When background compilation (via BackgroundCompileTask/LazyCompileDispatcher) lazily parses a function that contains a super property expression (e.g., super.prop) and the enclosing scope already has a scope_info_ (i.e., a prior scope info from a previous parse), the function calls scope_info_->ContextSlotIndex(name->string(), &lookup_result). The name->string() call returns a Handle<String> that was created in the main thread's handle scope. V8's threading model prohibits background threads from dereferencing handles not owned by their LocalHeap; background threads may only dereference their own local or persistent handles. When the main thread's heap is in a 'parked' state, its objects can be relocated by the garbage collector. If a background thread dereferences a main-thread handle at this point, it may access a stale or relocated heap object, leading to type confusion. The bug is triggered with --stress-background-compile --parallel-compile-tasks-for-lazy flags when JavaScript code uses super property access inside methods of classes that undergo lazy background compilation.

fixes.evidence_text: merges approved for [https://crrev.com/c/5350482](<https://crrev.com/c/5350482>) please merge this fix to 12.3-lkgr and 12.2-lkgr at your earliest convenience
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/5350482 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
